title: SafeRide — Ride Hailing Platform
description: A ride-hailing kiosk and lightweight web app designed for people without smartphones, mobile data, or cash. Built as part of an agile team, I handled the full stack: front-end kiosk screens, back-end services for logins, payments, ride bookings and live trip tracking, and database integration. Focused on safety, usability, and reliability for underserved communities.

title: CPUT Clinic Booking System
description: An online clinic appointment system built to streamline healthcare access for CPUT students. I contributed to front-end booking interfaces, developed server-side appointment logic, and designed relational databases to securely store patient and booking data. Also handled testing, debugging, and deployment in collaboration with the team.

title: GUM — Student Marketplace
description: A mobile student marketplace app for CPUT students to buy, sell, and connect on campus. Built user authentication, listings, messaging, and transaction features. Worked across front-end, back-end, and database layers. Participated in requirements analysis, API integration, and iterative testing to ensure scalability and usability.

title: QueUp — Smart Queue Management
description: A smart queue management platform that lets South Africans join government queues remotely — Home Affairs, SASSA, SARS, hospitals, courts, and municipalities. No more wasted hours standing in line. Users join from anywhere and get notified when it's their turn. Live across Cape Town with 450+ active branches.

title: VoucherBridge — Multi-Bank Cash Voucher Wallet
description: South African banks — FNB, ABSA, Standard Bank, Capitec, Nedbank — each issue cash vouchers that can only be redeemed within their own ecosystem, cannot be combined, and require physical ATM visits to cash out. Millions of rands in vouchers go underutilized every year, leaving unbanked and underbanked South Africans without a simple way to access their funds digitally. VoucherBridge solves this by letting users redeem vouchers from any bank into a single unified digital wallet, aggregate balances across banks, make instant PayShap-style transfers using a Pay ID, deposit funds directly to any South African bank account, and convert vouchers from one bank into credit usable at another. Freemium model targeting the millions of South Africans who rely on voucher-based money transfers daily.

title: Pulse — Friend Location & Activity Tracker
description: A mobile-first React web app for South African university students, launching at CPUT in Cape Town. Combines real-time friend location tracking with activity status detection — think Life360 meets Uber's design language. Features a full Leaflet map with CartoDB Dark Matter tiles centered on the CPUT campus, friend pins as circular avatars with color-coded borders (green for active, amber for recently active, grey for inactive), and a draggable bottom sheet with scrollable friend cards. The killer feature — Pulse Check — is a full friend list sorted by real-time activity with glowing animations for active users. Includes a freemium model with Ghost Mode, exact last seen times, and location history locked behind Pulse Premium at R49/month or R399/year. Privacy-first with full user control over who sees their location.

title: BookMzi — South African Digital Book Platform
description: A South African digital book platform combining three models into one. Netflix-style subscription streaming at Basic (R99/mo) or Premium (R199/mo) gives readers unlimited access to eBooks, PDFs, and audiobooks, with an optional audiobook add-on at R50/mo. A Takealot-style marketplace lets books be purchased individually, with authors earning 70–80% royalties on direct sales, plus student bundles and print-on-demand. Most importantly, BookMzi is a self-publishing platform for underrepresented South African authors — underprivileged and emerging writers can upload, publish, and monetize their work, track reads, sales, and revenue through dedicated dashboards, and access optional paid services like editing, cover design, and featured placement. Democratizing book access for South African readers while empowering local authors to reach an audience and earn a living from their writing.
